subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Bee Stings and Anaphylaxis
description: A 60-second lesson on Bee Stings and Anaphylaxis.

hook.text: A single bee sting kills more people in the US each year than shark attacks, bears, and snakes combined.

buildup.text: Bee venom contains melittin, which destroys red blood cells and triggers pain and inflammation.

discovery.text: Most stings cause only local pain. In allergic individuals, the immune system overreacts massively.

twist.text: You can develop a bee allergy at any age—even after years of stings without problems.

climax.text: Carrying epinephrine and knowing when to use it is the difference between life and death.

punchline.text: The world's deadliest creature isn't a shark. It weighs less than a paperclip.

quiz.question: Can someone develop a bee allergy later in life?
options: Yes, at any age even after prior safe exposure
No, allergies are always present from birth
Only children can develop new allergies
